137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass
304	
305	    if tags is not None:
306	        assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
307	        tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
308	    else:
309	        tags = ["unsloth",]
310	    pass
311	    save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags
312	
313	    if ((save_method == "lora") or (save_method == "merged_4bit")) and push_to_hub:
314	        if token is None:
315	            raise RuntimeError(
316	                "Unsloth: Pushing to HF requires a token. Pass `token = 'hf_....'`\n"\
317	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens."
318	            )
319	        pass
320	
321	        if save_method == "lora":
322	            print("Unsloth: Saving LoRA adapters. Please wait...")
323	        elif save_method == "merged_4bit":
324	            print("Unsloth: Saving 4bit Bitsandbytes model. Please wait...")
325	        pass
326	
327	        # Update model tag
328	        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
329	            model, save_directory, token,
330	            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
331	            old_username = None, private = private,
332	        )
333	
334	        getattr(model, "original_push_to_hub", model.push_to_hub)\
335	        (
336	            repo_id            = save_directory,
337	            use_temp_dir       = use_temp_dir,
338	            commit_message     = commit_message,

Perfect! Now I have gathered comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed summary report of the unsloth save/export architecture.

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Structure Report: Save & Export Methods

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth repository, here's the complete structure of how model saving and export methods are implemented:

---

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Main repository path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/`

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Entry point, imports from save.py
├── save.py                     # CORE FILE: All save/export implementations (2,679 lines)
├── trainer.py                  # Training-related utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer helper functions
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template handling
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation utilities
├── models/                     # Model-specific implementations

│   └── dpo.py / llama4.py / qwen3_moe.py
├── kernels/                    # Fast dequantization kernels

tests/
├── saving/                     # Save/export tests
│   ├── test_unsloth_save.py    # Main save tests
│   ├── language_models/        # LM save tests
│   ├── vision_models/          # Vision model save tests
│   └── gpt-oss-merge/          # GPT merge tests
├── qlora/                      # QLoRA merge tests
└── test_model_registry.py
```

---

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT RELATED FILES

**Main implementation file:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/unsloth/save.py` (105,493 bytes)

**Test files:**

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/tests/saving/language_models/test_merge_model_perplexity_*.py`

---

### 3. KEY FUNCTIONS & CLASSES IN save.py

#### **A. Core Save Functions** (lines 205-2679)

**Main dispatcher function:**
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,

    # ... 20+ additional parameters
)
```
**Location:** Lines 205-762
**Purpose:** Central dispatcher that handles all three save modes (LoRA, merged_16bit, merged_4bit). Routes to appropriate handlers based on `save_method`.

---

#### **B. GGUF Export Functions**

**1. GGUF Save Method:**
```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type           : str,
    model_dtype          : str,
    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized",

    _run_installer = None,
)
```
**Location:** Lines 952-1320
**Purpose:** Converts merged models to GGUF format. Supports multiple quantization methods:
- `not_quantized`, `fast_quantized`, `quantized`
- `f32`, `bf16`, `f16`, `q8_0`
- `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q3_k_*`, `q2_k`, `q6_k`, `q3_k_xs`, etc.

**2. Save to GGUF (Model method):**

**Location:** Lines 1768-1950
**Purpose:** Instance method attached to model. Merges 4-bit to 16-bit, then converts to GGUF.

**3. Push GGUF to Hub:**

**Location:** Lines 1951-2128
**Purpose:** Saves to GGUF and pushes to HuggingFace Hub.

---

#### **C. Merged Model Saving Functions**

**Purpose:** Saves merged model (16-bit or 4-bit) locally. Delegates to `unsloth_save_model()`.

**2. Push Merged Models to Hub:**

**Purpose:** Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format and saves locally.

---

#### **E. TorchAO Quantization Saving**

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
```

**Location:** Lines 2502-2561
**Purpose:** Merges to 16-bit, then applies TorchAO quantization. Supports custom quantization configs like `Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()`.

---

#### **F. Generic Vision Model Saving**

```python
@torch.inference_mode
```

```python
def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(...)
```

---

#### **G. Patching & Method Attachment**

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
```
**Location:** Lines 2569-2678
**Purpose:** **CRITICAL**: This function dynamically attaches all save/export methods to the model instance using Python's `types.MethodType()`. 

**Methods added to model:**
- `model.push_to_hub_merged`
- `model.save_pretrained_merged`

### 4. HELPER FUNCTIONS

**Core merge logic:**


**Tokenizer utilities:**

---

### 5. QUANTIZATION METHODS & CONSTANTS

**Supported quantization types** (lines 79-107):
```python
ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
    "not_quantized", "fast_quantized", "quantized",
    "f32", "bf16", "f16",
    "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q2_k", "q3_k_l", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s",
    "q4_0", "q4_1", "q4_k_s", "q4_k", "q5_k", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q5_k_s",
```

**Model-specific weights to merge** (lines 67-75):

```python
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", 
    "self_attn.o_proj", "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj"

LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
```

---

### 6. IMPORTS & DEPENDENCIES

**From unsloth internals:**
```python
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias

```

**From external packages:**
```python
from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from unsloth_zoo.saving_utils import merge_and_overwrite_lora, prepare_saving
from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp import install_llama_cpp, convert_to_gguf

from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit, Linear as Peft_Linear
from huggingface_hub import HfApi, get_token
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
```

---

### 7. PUBLIC API (Exposed in __init__.py)

**Lines 48-54 of save.py define `__all__`:**
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

**Imported into main package** (line 263 of `__init__.py`):
```python
from .save import *  # Exports all of __all__
```

---

### 8. HOW METHODS ARE ATTACHED TO MODELS

**Pattern used in `patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2658-2678):**

```python
# For language models:
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)

# For vision models (limited options):
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
```

---

### 9. SAVE METHOD PATTERNS & FLOW

**Pattern 1: LoRA Saving**
```
unsloth_save_model(save_method="lora")
  → model.save_pretrained() with selected_adapters=None
  → Saves adapter_config.json + adapter_model.bin
```

**Pattern 2: Merged 16-bit Saving**
```
unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_16bit")
  → _merge_lora() on each layer
  → Dequantize 4-bit weights to float32
  → Convert to float16
  → Save as model.safetensors or model.bin
```

**Pattern 3: Merged 4-bit Saving**
```
unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_4bit")
  → model.merge_and_unload()
  → Quantize merged weights back to 4-bit
  → Save quantization_config in config.json
```

**Pattern 4: GGUF Export**
```
unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()
  → unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_16bit")
  → Install llama.cpp (non-blocking)
  → convert-hf-to-gguf.py (16-bit GGUF)
  → llama-quantize (apply chosen quantization)
  → Push to Hub if requested
```

**Pattern 5: TorchAO Quantization**
```
unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()
  → unsloth_generic_save(save_method="merged_16bit")
  → torchao.quantize_() with custom config
  → Save with safety_serialization=False
```

---

### 10. KEY STRUCTURAL INSIGHTS FOR ADDING ONNX EXPORT

Based on the codebase analysis, here's how to add ONNX export following unsloth patterns:

**Location:** Add new functions to `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/unsloth/save.py`

**Functions to create:**

1. **Core ONNX converter** (similar to `save_to_gguf`):
```python
def save_to_onnx(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: str = "unsloth_onnx_model",
    opset_version: int = 14,
    use_external_data_format: bool = True,  # For models > 2GB
    # ... optimization options
)
```

2. **Model method** (similar to `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf`):
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer = None,
    opset_version: int = 14,
    use_external_data_format: bool = True,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    # ... additional parameters
)
```

3. **Hub pushing method** (similar to `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf`):
```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_onnx(
    self,
    repo_id: str,
    tokenizer = None,
    # ... parameters
)
```

4. **Register in patch_saving_functions()** (line 2658+):
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)
model.push_to_hub_onnx     = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_onnx, model)
```

5. **Key implementation considerations:**
   - Pre-merge 4-bit to 16-bit (like GGUF does)
   - Use transformers' ONNX export utilities
   - Handle tokenizer export separately
   - Support quantization formats (e.g., int8, int16)
   - Create model/ort-model configuration
   - Use `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` for generic models
   - Add to `__all__` exports
   - Create tests in `/tests/saving/`

---

### 11. TEST PATTERNS

See `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` for patterns:

```python
def test_save_merged_16bit(model, tokenizer, temp_save_dir):
    model.save_pretrained_merged(save_path, tokenizer=tokenizer, save_method="merged_16bit")
    # Verify files exist, check configs, test loading

def test_save_merged_4bit(model, tokenizer, temp_save_dir):
    model.save_pretrained_merged(save_path, tokenizer=tokenizer, save_method="merged_4bit_forced")
    # Verify file sizes, quantization config

def test_save_torchao(fp16_model_tokenizer, temp_save_dir):
```

Perfect! Now I have gathered comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed summary report of the unsloth save/export architecture.

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Structure Report: Save & Export Methods

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth repository, here's the complete structure of how model saving and export methods are implemented:

---

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Main repository path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/`

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Entry point, imports from save.py
├── save.py                     # CORE FILE: All save/export implementations (2,679 lines)
├── trainer.py                  # Training-related utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer helper functions
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template handling
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation utilities
├── models/                     # Model-specific implementations
│   ├── loader.py              # Model loading logic
│   ├── loader_utils.py        # Loader utilities with get_model_name()
│   ├── _utils.py              # Utility functions
│   ├── llama.py               # Llama model support
│   ├── mistral.py             # Mistral model support
│   ├── gemma.py / gemma2.py   # Gemma model support
│   ├── qwen2.py / qwen3.py    # Qwen model support
│   ├── falcon_h1.py           # Falcon model support
│   ├── granite.py             # Granite model support
│   ├── vision.py              # Vision model support
│   ├── cohere.py              # Cohere model support
│   ├── rl.py / rl_replacements.py  # RL-specific code
│   └── dpo.py / llama4.py / qwen3_moe.py
├── kernels/                    # Fast dequantization kernels
├── registry/                   # Model registry
├── utils/                      # Utility functions
└── dataprep/                   # Data preparation

tests/
├── saving/                     # Save/export tests
│   ├── test_unsloth_save.py    # Main save tests
│   ├── language_models/        # LM save tests
│   ├── vision_models/          # Vision model save tests
│   └── gpt-oss-merge/          # GPT merge tests
├── qlora/                      # QLoRA merge tests
└── test_model_registry.py
```

---

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT RELATED FILES

**Main implementation file:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/unsloth/save.py` (105,493 bytes)

**Test files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/tests/saving/language_models/test_merge_model_perplexity_*.py`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/tests/saving/language_models/test_push_to_hub_merged*.py`

---

### 3. KEY FUNCTIONS & CLASSES IN save.py

#### **A. Core Save Functions** (lines 205-2679)

**Main dispatcher function:**
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    # ... 20+ additional parameters
)
```
**Location:** Lines 205-762
**Purpose:** Central dispatcher that handles all three save modes (LoRA, merged_16bit, merged_4bit). Routes to appropriate handlers based on `save_method`.

---

#### **B. GGUF Export Functions**

**1. GGUF Save Method:**
```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type           : str,
    model_dtype          : str,
    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    _run_installer = None,
)
```
**Location:** Lines 952-1320
**Purpose:** Converts merged models to GGUF format. Supports multiple quantization methods:
- `not_quantized`, `fast_quantized`, `quantized`
- `f32`, `bf16`, `f16`, `q8_0`
- `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q3_k_*`, `q2_k`, `q6_k`, `q3_k_xs`, etc.

**2. Save to GGUF (Model method):**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    # ... additional parameters
)
```
**Location:** Lines 1768-1950
**Purpose:** Instance method attached to model. Merges 4-bit to 16-bit, then converts to GGUF.

**3. Push GGUF to Hub:**
```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    # ... additional parameters
)
```
**Location:** Lines 1951-2128
**Purpose:** Saves to GGUF and pushes to HuggingFace Hub.

---

#### **C. Merged Model Saving Functions**

**1. Save Merged Models (Model method):**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit",
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    # ... additional parameters
)
```
**Location:** Lines 1321-1361
**Purpose:** Saves merged model (16-bit or 4-bit) locally. Delegates to `unsloth_save_model()`.

**2. Push Merged Models to Hub:**
```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit",
    # ... additional parameters
)
```
**Location:** Lines 1364-1407
**Purpose:** Saves merged model and pushes to HuggingFace Hub.

---

#### **D. Adapter/LoRA Saving Functions**

**1. Save LoRA to Custom Directory:**
```python
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
```
**Location:** Lines 2129-2140
**Purpose:** Saves LoRA adapters to disk using `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="lora"`.

**2. Convert LoRA to GGML and Push to Hub:**
```python
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    # ... additional parameters
)
```
**Location:** Lines 2143-2208
**Purpose:** Saves LoRA adapters, converts to GGML format using llama.cpp, pushes to Hub.

**3. Convert LoRA to GGML Locally:**
```python
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: str,
    # ... additional parameters
)
```
**Location:** Lines 2211-2275
**Purpose:** Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format and saves locally.

---

#### **E. TorchAO Quantization Saving**

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
)
```
**Location:** Lines 2502-2561
**Purpose:** Merges to 16-bit, then applies TorchAO quantization. Supports custom quantization configs like `Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()`.

---

#### **F. Generic Vision Model Saving**

```python
@torch.inference_mode
def save_to_gguf_generic(
    model,
    save_directory,
    tokenizer,
    quantization_method = None,
    quantization_type = "Q8_0",
    repo_id = None,
    token = None,
)
```
**Location:** Lines 2276-2355
**Purpose:** Generic GGUF saving for vision and other model types. Uses external unsloth_zoo utilities.

```python
def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(...)
def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(...)
```
**Location:** Lines 2413-2500
**Purpose:** Generic merged saving for non-standard architectures.

---

#### **G. Patching & Method Attachment**

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
```
**Location:** Lines 2569-2678
**Purpose:** **CRITICAL**: This function dynamically attaches all save/export methods to the model instance using Python's `types.MethodType()`. 

**Methods added to model:**
- `model.push_to_hub_merged`
- `model.save_pretrained_merged`
- `model.push_to_hub_gguf`
- `model.save_pretrained_gguf`
- `model.save_pretrained_torchao`
- `model.push_to_hub_ggml` (LoRA only)
- `model.save_pretrained_ggml` (LoRA only)
- `model.push_to_hub` (overridden with unsloth wrapper)

---

### 4. HELPER FUNCTIONS

**Core merge logic:**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):  # Lines 159-187
    # Merges LoRA weights into base weights
    # Returns merged weight tensor and bias
```

**Weight saving:**
```python
def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):  # Lines 190-200
    # Optimized saving for low-CPU systems
```

**LoRA parameter extraction:**
```python
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
    # Lines 29, 164
    # Extracts A, B, s from LoRA weights
```

**Tokenizer utilities:**
```python
def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location)  # Lines 116-134
def fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)  # Lines 1586-1617
def fix_sentencepiece_gguf(tokenizer)  # Imported from tokenizer_utils.py
```

**HuggingFace Hub utilities:**
```python
def upload_to_huggingface(...)  # Lines 1495-1585

def _determine_username(...)  # Lines 1436-1454
```

**llama.cpp installation:**
```python
def install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()  # Lines 763-769
def install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()  # Lines 770-806
def install_python_non_blocking(packages=[])  # Lines 807-813
def try_execute(commands, force_complete=False)  # Lines 814-836
def get_executable(executables)  # Lines 937-951
```

---

### 5. QUANTIZATION METHODS & CONSTANTS

**Supported quantization types** (lines 79-107):
```python
ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
    "not_quantized", "fast_quantized", "quantized",
    "f32", "bf16", "f16",
    "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q2_k", "q3_k_l", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s",
    "q4_0", "q4_1", "q4_k_s", "q4_k", "q5_k", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q5_k_s",
    "q6_k", "q3_k_xs"
}
```

**Model-specific weights to merge** (lines 67-75):
```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", 
    "self_attn.o_proj", "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj"
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm"
)
```

---

### 6. IMPORTS & DEPENDENCIES

**From unsloth internals:**
```python
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from .models.loader_utils import get_model_name
```

**From external packages:**
```python
from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from unsloth_zoo.saving_utils import merge_and_overwrite_lora, prepare_saving
from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp import install_llama_cpp, convert_to_gguf
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit, Linear as Peft_Linear
from huggingface_hub import HfApi, get_token
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
```

---

### 7. PUBLIC API (Exposed in __init__.py)

**Lines 48-54 of save.py define `__all__`:**
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

**Imported into main package** (line 263 of `__init__.py`):
```python
from .save import *  # Exports all of __all__
```

---

### 8. HOW METHODS ARE ATTACHED TO MODELS

**Pattern used in `patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2658-2678):**

```python
# For language models:
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)

# For vision models (limited options):
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
```

---

### 9. SAVE METHOD PATTERNS & FLOW

**Pattern 1: LoRA Saving**
```
unsloth_save_model(save_method="lora")
  → model.save_pretrained() with selected_adapters=None
  → Saves adapter_config.json + adapter_model.bin
```

**Pattern 2: Merged 16-bit Saving**
```
unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_16bit")
  → _merge_lora() on each layer
  → Dequantize 4-bit weights to float32
  → Convert to float16
  → Save as model.safetensors or model.bin
```

**Pattern 3: Merged 4-bit Saving**
```
unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_4bit")
  → model.merge_and_unload()
  → Quantize merged weights back to 4-bit
  → Save quantization_config in config.json
```

**Pattern 4: GGUF Export**
```
unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()
  → unsloth_save_model(save_method="merged_16bit")
  → Install llama.cpp (non-blocking)
  → convert-hf-to-gguf.py (16-bit GGUF)
  → llama-quantize (apply chosen quantization)
  → Push to Hub if requested
```

**Pattern 5: TorchAO Quantization**
```
unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()
  → unsloth_generic_save(save_method="merged_16bit")
  → torchao.quantize_() with custom config
  → Save with safety_serialization=False
```

---

### 10. KEY STRUCTURAL INSIGHTS FOR ADDING ONNX EXPORT

Based on the codebase analysis, here's how to add ONNX export following unsloth patterns:

**Location:** Add new functions to `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/unsloth/save.py`

**Functions to create:**

1. **Core ONNX converter** (similar to `save_to_gguf`):
```python
def save_to_onnx(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: str = "unsloth_onnx_model",
    opset_version: int = 14,
    use_external_data_format: bool = True,  # For models > 2GB
    # ... optimization options
)
```

2. **Model method** (similar to `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf`):
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer = None,
    opset_version: int = 14,
    use_external_data_format: bool = True,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    # ... additional parameters
)
```

3. **Hub pushing method** (similar to `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf`):
```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_onnx(
    self,
    repo_id: str,
    tokenizer = None,
    # ... parameters
)
```

4. **Register in patch_saving_functions()** (line 2658+):
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx, model)
model.push_to_hub_onnx     = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_onnx, model)
```

5. **Key implementation considerations:**
   - Pre-merge 4-bit to 16-bit (like GGUF does)
   - Use transformers' ONNX export utilities
   - Handle tokenizer export separately
   - Support quantization formats (e.g., int8, int16)
   - Create model/ort-model configuration
   - Use `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` for generic models
   - Add to `__all__` exports
   - Create tests in `/tests/saving/`

---

### 11. TEST PATTERNS

See `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775328932994-r8yquo/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` for patterns:

```python
def test_save_merged_16bit(model, tokenizer, temp_save_dir):
    model.save_pretrained_merged(save_path, tokenizer=tokenizer, save_method="merged_16bit")
    # Verify files exist, check configs, test loading

def test_save_merged_4bit(model, tokenizer, temp_save_dir):
    model.save_pretrained_merged(save_path, tokenizer=tokenizer, save_method="merged_4bit_forced")
    # Verify file sizes, quantization config

def test_save_torchao(fp16_model_tokenizer, temp_save_dir):
    model.save_pretrained_torchao(save_path, tokenizer=tokenizer, torchao_config=...)
    # Verify quantization applied
```

---

This comprehensive analysis provides a complete roadmap for understanding and extending the unsloth save/export system. The architecture is highly modular, following a consistent pattern of core functions, model methods, hub pushing variants, and patch registration.

    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly

def print_quantization_methods():
    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():

    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass


@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

        logger.warning_once(

        )
    pass

    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = True
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            python_install.wait()
        pass
    else:
        try:
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        except:
            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp
            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
                # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
                python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
                python_install.wait()
                install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
                new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
                makefile = None
            else:
                git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
                python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
                git_clone.wait()
                makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()

tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

    merge_and_overwrite_lora(
        get_model_name,
        model                = model,
        tokenizer            = tokenizer,
        save_directory       = save_directory,
        push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
        private              = private,
        token                = token,
        save_method          = save_method,
        output_dtype         = None,
        low_disk_space_usage = True,
        use_temp_file        = False,
    )
    return
pass


def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.

content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me understand what I need to add:

1. A core `export_lora_to_onnx` function that:
   - Iterates over the model's named modules
   - Identifies LoRA layers (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear, Bnb_Linear4bit)
   - Extracts A matrix, B matrix, and scaling (s)
   - Extracts metadata (rank = A.shape[0], alpha = s * rank based on PEFT convention, target module name)
   - Creates ONNX nodes for each adapter by multiplying A and B matrices
   - Saves the result to output_dir with metadata JSON

2. An `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` method bound to the model, register it in `patch_saving_functions`, and add it to `__all__`.

For the ONNX representation, I need to think through the LoRA math: the output update is (B @ A) * scaling * x, where A is [r, in_features] and B is [out_features, r], and the scaling factor follows PEFT's convention of lora_alpha / r.

I'm considering two approaches: either export each adapter as its own small ONNX graph containing just the A and B matrices with scaling, or combine all adapters into a single ONNX model. Since merging base model weights into ONNX would require the full model, the cleaner approach is to export the adapter computation separately—the ONNX graph takes the layer input and produces the delta (B @ A @ input * scaling) that can be added to the base output. Building the ONNX graph structure... @ A.T gives me [-1, r], then multiplying by B.T yields [-1, out_features], and scaling brings me to the final shape. Alternatively, I could precompute the weight delta directly as B @ A scaled, then export it as a constant or single operation, but computing it as a graph is more idiomatic for ONNX. Now I'm setting up the export function...

Writing export logic... For each LoRA-adapted module, produces a separate ONNX graph that represents the adapter computation: delta = (x @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T) * scaling. Also writes an adapter_metadata.json file with rank, alpha and target module information so the graphs can be re-identified at inference time.

Now I'm handling the ONNX import with a fallback error message, then setting up the output directory and initializing metadata with format, opset version, and adapter tracking. I'm checking if the model has PEFT configuration and extracting the target modules from it... Extracting LoRA weight matrices...

Now I'm extracting the dimensions from the weight matrices and recovering the alpha scaling factor from the stored scaling value. I'm also sanitizing the module name to create a valid filename for the ONNX export, then setting up the computation graph that will apply the LoRA transformation: multiplying the input by the A matrix transpose, then by the B matrix transpose, and finally scaling the result.

Setting up the ONNX initializers... Building the ONNX graph...

Now I'm setting the model's documentation string with the LoRA parameters and IR version, then embedding the rank, alpha, scaling factor, and module name as metadata properties on the model proto.

Finishing the metadata setup... Writing the export function... Opset 17 is the minimum required version to support all the operators being used, though I can increase it for newer runtimes. The function then calls the export helper, and if a tokenizer was provided, it saves that alongside the ONNX files and prints a confirmation message.

If no tokenizer is provided, I log a warning suggesting the user can save it separately. After that, I'm running garbage collection a few times to clean up memory. I also need to register this new method in the patching function and update the module's public API exports.

Now I'm planning out where to insert these functions into the actual save.py file—the ONNX export function goes after the GGML conversion code, the save method goes after the torchao saving function, and I need to wire everything up through the patching system and exports.
